content.Text: [Sun 2026-04-19 13:12 CDT] You are updating TWO GA4 dashboard HTML files. Work carefully and completely on both files. --- ## FILE 1: `/Users/lucfaucheux/.openclaw/workspace/ga4-dashboards/audi-baton-rouge-dashboard.html` ### 1a. Update DATA object with REAL numbers (replace existing values): ``` last30: sessions:16279, users:14030, newUsers:11311, pageviews:38059 prev30: sessions:13993, users:11796, newUsers:9522, pageviews:39173 last60: sessions:30272, users:25826, newUsers:20833, pageviews:77232 prev60: sessions:24491, users:20789, newUsers:16421, pageviews:57293 q1_2026 (90-day): sessions:42974, users:36530, newUsers:29194, pageviews:109189 yoy30: sessions:18293, users:15633, newUsers:12462, pageviews:40328 yoy60: sessions:36935, users:31512, newUsers:25274, pageviews:84220 q1_2025 / yoy90: sessions:55398, users:47744, newUsers:38920, pageviews:124617 ``` Keep pagesPerSession and daily chart arrays as-is. ### 1b. Fix ALL dataset label properties: - last30.label → `"Last 30 Days"` - prev30.label → `"Previous 30 Days"` - last60.label → `"Last 60 Days"` - prev60.label → `"Previous 60 Days"` - q1_2026.label → `"Last 90 Days"` - yoy30.label → `"Same 30 Days Last Year"` - yoy60.label → `"Same 60 Days Last Year"` - q1_2025.label → `"Same 90 Days Last Year"` (rename the object key to `yoy90` as well if feasible, otherwise just fix the label) ### 1c. Fix the YoY compare button: Change button text from `"vs Q1 2025"` (or whatever it currently says) to `"vs Prior Year"` ### 1d. Fix getCompareData() function: ```javascript function getCompareData(){ if(compare==='none') return null; if(compare==='yoy') return range===90 ? DATA.yoy90 : range===60 ? DATA.yoy60 : DATA.yoy30; return range===30 ? DATA.prev30 : range===60 ? DATA.prev60 : DATA.q1_2026_prev; // prev 90 } ``` IMPORTANT: the `prev` path for range=90 must return the PREVIOUS 90-day period data (NOT yoy data). Check the file — find whatever object holds the previous 90-day period and use that. If there's no separate prev90 object and q1_2025 was being used as both prev and yoy, you'll need to create a separate entry or use q1_2025 only for prev and yoy90 for the yoy path. ### 1e. Add `.card-compare` CSS (if not already present): In the `

Let me start with the edits. The ABR file has a tricky issue—it references `DATA.q1_2024` which doesn't exist, and the getCompareData function needs fixing. For the 90-day range, there's no separate prev90 data, so I need to keep q1_2025 as the yoy90 data while handling the previous period correctly.

Looking at what the user provided, q1_2025 is marked as yoy90 data, but for the actual previous 90-day period (Oct-Dec 2025), there's no separate object in the file. I need to figure out whether to create a new entry or repurpose what's available, since q1_2025 can't serve double duty as both the previous period and the year-over-year comparison. Looking at the getCompareData function, I see it references `DATA.q1_2026_prev` for the 90-day previous comparison, but that dataset wasn't provided. The prev30 and prev60 data exist, so prev90 should represent the 90 days before the current period—October through December 2025—which we don't have in the data.

The most practical fix is to derive a `prev90` entry by scaling up the prev60 data proportionally to cover the full 90-day window, since that's the only historical data available for that comparison. I'm re-reading the user's instruction about handling the data structure. They're saying to use q1_2025 for the prev path and yoy90 for the year-over-year comparison, where both reference the same underlying data from Q1 2025. So I'd rename q1_2025 to yoy90 and use that same dataset for both the previous period lookup and the YoY calculation.

But wait—the user's template shows a specific pattern with prev30, prev60, and a third entry for the 90-day range. I need to create that q1_2026_prev entry, but the user never provided actual previous 90-day data for ABR, so I'm stuck on what values to populate it with. I'm realizing I don't actually have the previous 90-day data for October-December 2025 that the user didn't provide, so I should just stick with the structure they gave me and use the year-over-year data as the best available fallback for comparison purposes. at what BMW Jackson does - it also uses `DATA.q1_2025` for both prev and yoy at 90 days (line 463-468). And the user wants me to fix that too.

For BMW Jackson, the user says "Fix getCompareData() — same logic, make sure prev and yoy return different datasets for range=90". But BMW Jackson also doesn't have prev90 data!

OK here's what I'll do: For both files, I'll make the prev path at range=90 return null (no comparison) since we don't have prev90 data. No wait, that would break the UI.

Alright, I'll just use yoy90 for the prev path at 90 too. The label on yoy90 says "Same 90 Days Last Year" which is different from "Previous 90 Days", so at least the labels will be correct for the yoy path. But for prev at 90, both paths return the same data with different UI labels, which is confusing.

Actually, I just realized - for ABR, the user renamed q1_2025 label to "Same 90 Days Last Year". If prev at 90 also returns this object, the card will say "vs Same 90 Days Last Year" which doesn't make sense for a "vs Previous Period" button. I could create a prev90 entry that's a copy of yoy90 but with the label "Previous 90 Days" so the UI is consistent, but then the user will see identical numbers for both comparison modes at 90 days, which is also wrong.

Let me just follow the temp...
